sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Juventus, sitting at 4th in the league with 63 points, face bottom-placed Monza, who are 20th with just 15 points, in Round 34 at the Allianz Stadium in Turin. This matchup pits a strong contender against a struggling side, with Juventus holding a significant advantage based on league standings. The referee, Daniele Perenzoni, averages 5.2 yellow cards per game and 0.31 red cards per game, suggesting a potentially feisty encounter. Given the disparity in form and position, this game could be a defining moment for both teams’ seasons.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: In the last 5 matchups between these two sides, Juventus have dominated with 4 wins, while Monza managed just 1 victory and no draws. The most recent encounter on December 22, 2024, saw Juventus triumph 2-1 away at Monza, with Stefano Turati earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive 8.4 Sofascore rating. Head-to-head trends show Juventus winning their last 3 games against Monza, often scoring first in 4 of the last 5 meetings, while Monza have failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 3 encounters against the Bianconeri.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Juventus come into this game with a mixed recent record, having gone without a clean sheet in their last 3 home games, though this streak has ended. Meanwhile, Monza are in dire straits, with 4 consecutive losses and a winless streak of 13 matches, alongside failing to keep a clean sheet in their last 8 away games. Juventus have a tendency to play in matches with less than 10.5 corners in 5 of their last 7 home games, a trend mirrored by Monza in 8 of their last 10 away games. These streaks highlight Juventus’ relative stability compared to Monza’s ongoing struggles.

sections.subtitle: Juventus Key Players and Stats
sections.text: Juventus boast a strong lineup with Yıldıız, Kenan, a midfielder ranked 4th in the league for successful dribbles per game at 1.7, leading the charge with 6 goals and an in-form average Sofascore rating of 7.525 over the last 5 matches. Randal Kolo Muani, a forward with 6 goals, has overperformed his expected goals of 3.46 by 2.54, showing clinical finishing. Khéphren Thuram also stands out with 5 assists and a solid 7.07 average rating. Nicolòs González, a standout in past matchups against Monza, averages a 7.6 Sofascore rating with 3 goals in these games. Juventus’ season stats are impressive, with 52 goals scored, 16 clean sheets (ranked 2nd), and a league-leading 88.2% accurate passes, showcasing their dominance in possession at 57.8% (3rd in the league).

sections.subtitle: Monza Key Players and Stats
sections.text: Monza rely on forward Dany Mota, who has 5 goals this season, matching his expected goals of 5.03, alongside 2 assists and a 6.91 average rating. Georgios Kyriakopoulos adds creativity from midfield with 4 assists and a 6.93 rating, while Samuele Birindelli contributes with 2 goals. However, Monza’s overall form is poor, with only 4 goals scored in the 1-15 minute period and a mere 21 goals for the season based on their goal distribution. Their defense has struggled, conceding consistently across all game periods, and they lack any in-form players in the last 5 matches.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Missing Players
sections.text: Juventus will miss key forward Dušan Vlahović, who has 9 goals and 4 assists, alongside midfielder Teun Koopmeiners with 3 goals and 3 assists, potentially impacting their attacking depth. Monza, on the other hand, are without defenders Armando Izzo and Danilo D'Ambrosio, as well as midfielder Matteo Pessina, further weakening their already fragile squad. Juventus’ ability to create big chances (75, ranked 8th) and their penalty area entries per game (25.26, ranked 10th) could exploit Monza’s defensive frailties. Monza’s goal distribution shows vulnerability late in halves, conceding 25.4% of goals between minutes 31-45, which Juventus could target with their 21.9% of goals scored in the same period.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Trends
sections.text: Juventus have the league’s best opponent big chances created stat, allowing only 39 such opportunities, ranked 1st, underlining their defensive solidity. Their 16 clean sheets (ranked 2nd) contrast sharply with Monza’s inability to keep a clean sheet in recent games. Juventus hit the woodwork 12 times this season (ranked 8th for unluckiest), suggesting they could have scored even more than their 52 goals. Meanwhile, Monza’s matches often see less than 4.5 cards in 8 of their last 10 away games, and head-to-head games with Juventus have consistently had under 4.5 cards in all 7 recent meetings. Juventus also score 21.9% of their goals in the final 15 minutes (76-90), potentially a critical period in this game.
